revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:千葉縣護國神社]]
{{Unreferenced|date=August 28, 2017|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Chiba Gokoku Shrine | image = File:ChibakenGokokuJinja20100501.jpg | coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|12|N|140|06|42|E||qq=}} | location = 4-1-1 Sakura-gi, {{ill|Wakaba-ku|en|Wakaba-ku|qid=Q1075889|lt=Wakaba Ward|1=Wakaba-ku}}, {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|qid=Q170616|lt=Chiba City|1=Chiba (city)}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} | deity = War dead from Chiba Prefecture | founded = 1878 (Meiji 11) | type = Ministry of Home Affairs Designated Gokoku Shrine・Special Shrine | festival = April 10・October 10 | latDeg = 35|LatMin = 37|LatSec = 12|LonDeg = 140|LonMin = 06|LonSec = 42|map = Japan Chiba|qq=}} '''Chiba Prefectural Gokoku Shrine''' (千葉縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Wakaba-ku|en|Wakaba-ku|qid=Q1075889|lt=Wakaba Ward|1=Wakaba-ku}}, {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|qid=Q170616|lt=Chiba City|1=Chiba (city)}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. The shrine crest is a circle with a cherry blossom. == Overview == The shrine honors war dead from {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. As of February 2022, the shrine enshrines 57,828 spirits.<ref name="sankei20220226">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20220226-BMMV37MZ55PC7MYDLOZG5RDP5E/ |title=Chiba Prefectural Gokoku Shrine relocation, consideration for bereaved families with barrier-free access |website=Sankei News |publisher=Sankei Digital |date=February 26, 2022 |accessdate=June 8, 2022 |qq=}}</ref>. == History == The shrine was founded on January 27, 1878 (Meiji 11) as the '''Chiba Prefectural Shōkonsha''' by the initiative of the first prefectural governor, {{ill|Shibahara Kazu|ja|柴原和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibahara Kazu|1=Shibahara Yawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11534638}}. It initially enshrined 16 spirits, including {{ill|Adachi Naotsugu|ja|安達直次郎盛篤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Naotsugu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the {{ill|Sakura Domain|en|Sakura Domain|qid=Q910993|lt=Sakura Domain|1=Sakura Domain}}, who died in the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}. In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed '''Chiba Prefectural Gokoku Shrine''' following the reform of the Shōkonsha system to Gokoku Shrines. On April 19, 1943 (Shōwa 18), it was designated as the one Gokoku Shrine for the prefecture by the competent minister. Originally located within the {{ill|Chiba Prefectural Government|ja|千葉県庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefectural Government|1=Chiba Prefectural Government|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21666178}} park, the shrine was relocated to the precincts of {{ill|Chiba Shrine|en|Chiba Shrine|qid=Q11406700|lt=Chiba Shrine|1=Chiba Shrine}} in 1889 (Meiji 22). Due to the limited space, it was relocated again to {{ill|Inohana|ja|亥鼻山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inohana|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1937 (Shōwa 12). The new shrine buildings were completed in 1943 (Shōwa 18), and a dedication ceremony was held on April 19, 1943. However, the shrine was destroyed in a bombing raid that devastated much of Chiba City in July 1945 (Shōwa 20). After World War II, the shrine was renamed Shōtoku Shrine to avoid being associated with militarism. It reverted to its original name, Chiba Prefectural Gokoku Shrine, in 1952 (Shōwa 27) following the restoration of Japan's sovereignty. On September 30, 1967 (Shōwa 42), the shrine was relocated from Inohana to Benten, Chūō Ward.<ref name="sankei20210813">{{Cite web|language=Japanese|url=https://www.sankei.com/article/20210813-66QSVTDWOFPSNFN3JEWMQDOP2Q/ |title=Chiba Prefectural Gokoku Shrine to relocate in February next year, consideration for elderly bereaved families with barrier-free access |website=Sankei News |publisher=Sankei Digital |date=August 13, 2021 |accessdate=June 8, 2022 |qq=}}</ref>. In 1996 (Heisei 8), the shrine was listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. On October 13, 1973 (Shōwa 48), the shrine was visited by {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|qid=Q34479|lt=Emperor Shōwa|1=Hirohito}}, {{ill|Empress Nagako|en|Empress Nagako|qid=Q230697|lt=Empress Kōjun|1=Empress Nagako}}, {{ill|Akihito|en|Akihito|qid=Q37979|lt=Crown Prince Akihito|1=Akihito}}, and {{ill|Crown Princess Michiko|ja|美智子妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Princess Michiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上皇后美智子|1=Empress Michiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q87610}}, who paid their respects in conjunction with the opening ceremony of the {{ill|28th National Sports festival|ja|第28回国民体育大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/28th National Sports festival|1=28th National Sports festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11600725}}. In October 1978 (Shōwa 53), the shrine's centennial was celebrated with a grand festival held in conjunction with the spring festival. A monument to the special attack forces was erected in May 2011 (Heisei 23). Due to the aging of the bereaved families and the deterioration of the buildings<ref name="sankei20210813"/>, the shrine was relocated from Benten 3-chome 16-1, Chūō Ward to its current location in February 2022 (Reiwa 4)<ref name="sankei20220226"/>. == location == 4-1-1 Sakura-gi, {{ill|Wakaba-ku|en|Wakaba-ku|qid=Q1075889|lt=Wakaba Ward|1=Wakaba-ku}}, {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|qid=Q170616|lt=Chiba City|1=Chiba (city)}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} == festivals == * '''Seasonal Grand festivals''' ** Spring (April 10) ** Autumn (October 10) * '''Mitama festival''' ** July 13-16 == References == {{Reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|qid=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} * {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Chibaken-Gokoku-jinja|qq=}} * [https://www.chiba-gokoku.jp/ Chiba Prefectural Gokoku Shrine] (Official Site) {{Normdaten|qq=}} {{Gokoku Shrines|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|千葉縣護國神社|version=101990693|comment=Imported full ja history}}
